sweep_summary.description: FF T1b hyperparameter sweep: coherent vs incoherent sequence discrimination

aggregation_comparison_ep500.conclusion: Mean-pooling is BEST aggregation, NOT the problem

seq_len_interaction.conclusion: Longer seq_len helps slightly, especially at low epochs

hidden_dim_effect_at_ep80.conclusion: SMALLER hidden dim is better at low epochs (less overfitting)

n_layers_effect_at_ep80.conclusion: More layers slightly help, but marginal

verdict.mechanism: FF learns to discriminate mean-pooled coherent vs incoherent sequences. The mean of a coherent sequence (all tokens from same cluster) maps to a tighter region than the mean of an incoherent sequence (tokens from different clusters). FF needs sufficient epochs (300+) to learn this discrimination.
verdict.root_cause_of_baseline_failure: Undertraining (80 epochs was ~4x too few). At 80 epochs, the FF layers have not converged enough to separate the mean-pooled distributions.
verdict.recommendation: Use 500+ epochs for T1b. hidden=256, n_layers=3, lr=0.5 is sufficient. seq_len=32 gives best results.
